# Test stdio transport functionality

# Test mcpspy basic functionality
exec mcpspy --help
stderr 'Usage of mcpspy'

# Test timeout handling with aggressive timeout
env MCP_TOOL_TIMEOUT=2s

# Test stdin/stdout handling with piped input
exec bash -c 'echo "{\"jsonrpc\": \"2.0\", \"id\": 1, \"method\": \"initialize\", \"params\": {\"protocolVersion\": \"2025-03-26\"}}" | mcpspy --pretty'
stdout '"jsonrpc": "2.0"'
stdout '"method": "initialize"'
stdout '"protocolVersion": "2025-03-26"'

# Test multiple JSON objects in sequence
exec bash -c 'printf "%s\n%s\n" "{\"jsonrpc\": \"2.0\", \"id\": 1, \"method\": \"initialize\"}" "{\"jsonrpc\": \"2.0\", \"id\": 2, \"method\": \"tools/list\"}" | mcpspy --pretty'
stdout '"initialize"'
stdout '"tools/list"'

# Test empty input handling
exec bash -c 'echo "" | mcpspy --pretty'
# Should not crash
